title: Token stream routing is the canonical one-line description of AMS
content: Replaces the prior "stupid-simple foundation for agent-to-agent communication" framing. The new descriptor names what AMS does (routes token streams) without naming who uses it (agents) or how it's positioned (foundation). Vodka-correct: describes the function, not the consumer or the marketing posture. Applied as the standalone subtitle (with a period) in README.md, AMS.md, SPEC.md, and HORIZON.md. The "agent-to-agent" framing remains in deeper context where appropriate but is no longer the canonical description.

title: The durable-thread vision is documented as HORIZON.md, separate from SPEC.md
content: HORIZON.md captures the forward-looking essay about what AMS plus a TruthKit-shaped harness makes possible after the PoC ships: durable threads that survive dropped browser connections, cross-device continuity, generation-as-shared-artifact, real-time governance as a subscriber posture, agent crash recovery, and the eventual category collapse of "chat platform" into "AMS conversation plus a UI". It is explicitly NOT the spec — SPEC.md remains the contract. HORIZON.md is the dream the spec is the first move toward, kept in its own document so vision and contract do not bleed into each other. The spec stays falsifiable; the horizon stays generative.

title: The wedge for adoption is the felt pain (dropped connections), not the category shift (multi-agent)
content: The "response incomplete" red bar is the most universally felt LLM UX failure on the planet. People will adopt AMS-plus-harness for that fix alone. The multi-subscriber fabric, cross-device continuity, generation-as-artifact, and governance-as-subscriber affordances all come along free once the substrate exists. This means the wedge product does not have to advertise as multi-agent infrastructure (which most users do not want and do not understand) — it can advertise as "your generations don't get lost anymore" (which everyone wants). The category shift is what it actually is, but the felt pain is how it gets in the door.

title: The model providers themselves may ship native conversation resumption, weakening but not closing the wedge
content: OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google could add native streaming-response resumption with replay tomorrow. That would close the entry-point pain (the red bar) without solving the multi-subscriber category. It would weaken the AMS adoption wedge but leave most of the durable-thread territory open: cross-device continuity beyond a single provider's API, generation-as-shared-artifact across organizations, governance as a subscriber to the same stream, agent crash recovery across replaceable instances. The risk is real but not category-killing. Worth tracking as a watch item.

title: HORIZON.md is reshaped from essay to comprehensive use-case catalog
content: The first version of HORIZON.md was an essay centered on the dropped-connection wedge. Reshaped to a catalog of ~40 use cases organized across 9 categories — consumer UX, collaboration, multi-agent systems, governance, reliability, multi-modal, industry-specific (TruthKit verticals), developer tools, creative/educational. Each entry follows a uniform format: what it is, what AMS specifically enables, what's needed beyond AMS to ship it. The wedge insight is preserved as section 10. The architectural inversion (harness as durable, browser as re-attachable lens) is preserved as the framing section before the catalog. The point: the catalog is wide because the vodka discipline of the wire produces a substrate that is genuinely domain-agnostic; listing categories so we can see the shape, not promising to build all of them. Explicitly framed as a map of the design space, not a roadmap.

title: HORIZON catalog gains §5 Transformation/Encoding/Artifact Projection and §11 Composition
content: The catalog gained a new category of subscribers — translation, DOLCHE+H and custom encoding, citation injection, citation validation, real-time artifact projection, document construction — and a new section explicitly naming Composition as the architectural unlock. The §5 entries are subscribers that read the conversation and produce parallel outputs without modifying source streams: translation as separate language streams, encoding to memory stores, citation augmentation and validation, materialization of artifacts (markdown, code files, slide decks, dashboards) that grow as the conversation progresses. §11 Composition makes explicit that the actual unlock is not any single pattern but the simultaneous composition of many — fifteen-plus subscribers on one conversation, each independent, each replaceable, none knowing about the others. §2.4 also reshaped from "Meeting agent" to "Human meetings as conversation input" to make the transcription-as-input flow first-class, since meeting transcripts are themselves input streams that everything in the catalog can subscribe to.

title: The category-naming insight: adding capability is adding a subscriber
content: The architectural payoff that §11 names: in the AMS+harness shape, adding capability is adding a subscriber, removing it is removing a subscriber, replacing it is replacing a subscriber. The protocol does not have to grow. The chat-product surface does not have to grow. The model does not have to grow. Only the subscriber catalog grows. Every subscriber can be deployed by anyone — customer, vendor, open-source contributor — without coordinating with anyone else. This is what makes the substrate generative rather than static. Any single pattern is interesting; the composition of many on one wire is the category.
